content: Diabetes management after hospital discharge requires careful attention to blood glucose monitoring and medication adherence. Key guidelines include: 1) Blood glucose monitoring - check blood sugar levels as prescribed, typically before meals and at bedtime. Record all readings. 2) Medication timing - take diabetes medications at the same time each day. Never skip doses. 3) Meal planning - follow a consistent carbohydrate intake pattern. Eat meals at regular times. 4) Hypoglycemia awareness - recognize symptoms of low blood sugar including shakiness, sweating, confusion, and rapid heartbeat. Always carry fast-acting glucose. 5) Foot care - inspect feet daily for cuts, blisters, or redness. Wear proper footwear. 6) Follow-up care - attend all scheduled appointments with your healthcare provider and diabetes educator. 7) Sick day management - have a plan for managing blood sugar during illness. Contact your provider if blood sugar remains high or you cannot eat.

citationFormat.citationText: American Diabetes Association. (2023). Diabetes Post-Discharge Management. Retrieved from https://diabetes.org/diabetes/medication-management
